project.name: Neighborhood Cooling Study Protocol
project.purpose: Plan a documented field research protocol for comparing street-level cooling interventions before any study execution begins.

non_goals.title: Do not collect participant data, deploy sensors, or run fieldwork from ni.
Do not treat ni readiness as scientific peer review or ethics approval.
Do not add a dashboard, web UI, queue, or analysis runtime.

capabilities.title: Define the study question, hypothesis, sampling frame, and inclusion criteria.
Specify data handling, ethics, and participant-contact boundaries.
Define reproducible analysis and evidence requirements for the protocol.

requirements.title: The protocol must state the research question and hypothesis in falsifiable terms.
The sampling frame must define eligible street segments, observation windows, and exclusion criteria.
The protocol must separate public environmental observations from any human-subject interaction.
The protocol must require ethics review before any participant contact or personally identifying data collection.
The analysis plan must name variables, comparison method, reproducibility evidence, and reviewer acceptance criteria.

decisions.title: Treat the output as a document protocol, not a data collection or analysis runtime.
Use independent protocol review as readiness evidence.
Generate human-team and generic prompts after lock for research team handoff.

risks.title: Ambiguous sampling criteria could bias the study.
risks.mitigation: Require explicit inclusion and exclusion criteria plus an independent reviewer reproduction check before fieldwork.

risks.title: Participant privacy or ethics boundaries may be crossed accidentally.
risks.mitigation: Separate environmental observation from participant contact and require ethics review before collecting personal data.

risks.title: Analysis decisions may drift after observations are collected.
risks.mitigation: Lock variables, comparison method, exclusion criteria, and reproducibility evidence in the protocol before execution.

evaluations.title: Sampling reproducibility review.
evaluations.method: An independent reviewer uses the protocol to select eligible street segments from a supplied map and records mismatches.

evaluations.title: Ethics boundary review.
evaluations.method: A reviewer checks whether each planned data item is public environmental observation, participant contact, or personally identifying data.

evaluations.title: Analysis plan reproducibility review.
evaluations.method: A reviewer confirms that variables, comparison method, exclusion criteria, and evidence package are sufficient to rerun the analysis.
